disclaimer: For research literacy and catalog cross-reference only. Not medical advice, prescribing guidance, or an endorsement of any product. Regulatory status and indications vary by jurisdiction; many entries are not FDA-approved for human use. Verify every vial identity with certificates of analysis and licensed professionals. Reported benefits, timing, and cycling fields summarize literature, labels, or typical preclinical models only. They are not individualized instructions. Controlled drugs and prescription peptides require licensed supervision. synergisticWith links describe co-use or combination narratives in trials, labels, named stacks, or speculative mechanistic themes; they are not recommendations to combine agents. potentialApplications state person-centered benefit language for education only; evidenceNote on each line limits the claim. Not a promise of results for any catalog SKU. doseGuidelines summarize published label, trial, or animal literature about doses where effects were seen. They are not instructions to self-dose, convert animal doses, or match grey-market vials to pharmaceuticals. distinctiveQuality summarizes class reputation and discourse only; it is not a verified identity claim for grey-market SKUs, not individualized advice, and filter themes are educational groupings only. reportedBenefits lines are evidence-tier-labeled summaries from labels, trials, or bench literature; they are not individualized outcome promises for grey-market products.

wellnessCategoryIndex.appetite_satiety: Appetite, feeling full, and eating behavior research
wellnessCategoryIndex.body_composition: Body fat, muscle mass, and weight-related research (clinical or lab)
wellnessCategoryIndex.cardiovascular_metabolic: Heart health, blood pressure, cholesterol, and metabolic risk research
wellnessCategoryIndex.cognitive_mood: Memory, focus, anxiety, depression, or brain growth-factor research
wellnessCategoryIndex.energy_mitochondria: Cellular energy production, mitochondrial health, exercise capacity, and NAD+-related metabolism
wellnessCategoryIndex.gastrointestinal: Gut lining, digestion, and digestive organ research
wellnessCategoryIndex.immune_inflammation: Immune system function, inflammatory signaling, or natural defense peptides
wellnessCategoryIndex.longevity_cellular_aging: Cell aging, chromosome-tip maintenance, aging blood markers, or lifespan studies
wellnessCategoryIndex.recovery_tissue_repair: Wound healing, muscle and joint repair, new blood vessel growth, or tissue remodeling research
wellnessCategoryIndex.reproductive_endocrine: Reproductive hormones, fertility, sexual function, or sex-hormone pathway research
wellnessCategoryIndex.respiratory_airway: Lung, airway lining, and breathing-related research
wellnessCategoryIndex.skin_pigmentation_aesthetics: Skin color, tanning, skin aging, or cosmetic skin research
wellnessCategoryIndex.sleep_circadian: Sleep quality, body clock biology, or sedation-related research
wellnessCategoryIndex.supports_accessory: Mixing liquids, solvents, or compounding supplies without their own drug activity

synergyEvidenceLegend.clinical_fixed_combo_development: This combination has been studied or approved as a defined drug pairing in clinical trials or product labels.
synergyEvidenceLegend.co_packaged_definition: These components are built into a single named product in this catalog.
synergyEvidenceLegend.named_stack_mechanism_complement: A named retail product where the ingredients are described as working through different but complementary pathways (mostly based on lab science or theory, not large human trials).
synergyEvidenceLegend.co_packaged_or_parallel_components: The product explicitly bundles these ingredients together or sells them as matched separate items.
synergyEvidenceLegend.vendor_blend_definition: The vendor's marketing defines this blend in relation to other products they sell.
synergyEvidenceLegend.co_secretagogue_grey_literature: Growth-hormone-releasing agents combined in non-mainstream wellness discussions; no proven clinical benefit outside their individual approved uses.
synergyEvidenceLegend.pituitary_axis_combination_discourse: Discussed together for stimulating growth hormone release from the pituitary gland; quality of evidence varies.
synergyEvidenceLegend.named_stack_product: A named combination product that pairs specific active ingredients together.
synergyEvidenceLegend.traditional_co_use_practice: A historical pattern of using these together in certain medical traditions or communities, not part of standard Western guidelines.
synergyEvidenceLegend.hypothesized_mitochondrial_complement: Both target cellular energy systems; very few human studies testing them as a combination.
synergyEvidenceLegend.hypothesized_redox_mitochondrial_complem: Linked in wellness marketing narratives only; no strong combined trial evidence.
synergyEvidenceLegend.khavinson_course_marketing: Bioregulator products bundled together commercially; limited independent clinical trial support.
synergyEvidenceLegend.immune_thymus_theme_juxtaposition: Grouped by a shared immune-system theme, not by proven combination benefits.
synergyEvidenceLegend.innate_immunity_theme_speculative: Both relate to the body's first-line immune defenses; whether they work better together is unclear.
synergyEvidenceLegend.wellness_adjunct_folklore: Offered side by side on clinic menus; not proof that they work better together.

potentialApplicationEvidenceLegend.how_to_read: The 'personCenteredBenefit' field describes the help a person might be looking for in plain language; the 'evidenceNote' field states how strong the evidence actually is so you can tell marketing apart from proof.
potentialApplicationEvidenceLegend.not_outcomes: Listing a benefit does not mean this particular vial will produce it; what actually happens depends on product identity, dose, purity, and medical supervision.

doseGuidelinesLegend.clinical_exploratory: Small or specialized human studies; not a consumer dosing card.
doseGuidelinesLegend.compounded_practice: Wellness clinic or regional prescribing customs; wide variation and weak evidence for a universal effective dose.
doseGuidelinesLegend.evidence_note: The 'minimumEffectiveDoseNotes' field summarizes where an effect first appeared in the cited evidence tier (product label, major trial, early study, or animal work). It is not your personal dose and not a substitute for a prescriber or trial protocol.
doseGuidelinesLegend.not_applicable_peptide: This is a mixing liquid or solvent; talking about a minimum drug dose does not apply.
doseGuidelinesLegend.phase1_human: Tested only in healthy volunteers for safety and basic effects; the effective long-term dose is usually unknown.
doseGuidelinesLegend.pivotal_trials: Large, high-quality trials that defined the effective maintenance doses for a specific medical condition.
doseGuidelinesLegend.preclinical_animal: Tested in animals only; translating those doses to humans is uncertain.
doseGuidelinesLegend.regulatory_label: Official prescribing information approved by the FDA or another national drug regulator.
doseGuidelinesLegend.unknown_identity: The active ingredient is not confirmed; a minimum effective dose cannot be stated.

knownForThemeIndex.metabolic_incretins: Appetite, weight, and blood sugar products based on gut hormones like GLP-1, dual GIP/GLP-1, triple-action incretins, amylin, or GLP-1/glucagon combinations.
knownForThemeIndex.growth_hormone_axis: Peptides that stimulate the pituitary gland to release growth hormone; includes products marketed for reducing belly fat.
knownForThemeIndex.tissue_healing: Known for repair and recovery of tendons, muscles, gut lining, or wounds (usually one main mechanism per product).
knownForThemeIndex.multi_ingredient_stack: A vendor-prepared blend of multiple active ingredients where the selling point is the bundle, not any single ingredient.
knownForThemeIndex.skin_tanning_libido: Products for skin darkening, tanning, melanin pathways, or sexual arousal through melanocortin receptors.
knownForThemeIndex.mitochondria_nad_redox: Products targeting cellular energy factories (mitochondria), NAD+ levels, or major cellular antioxidant systems.
knownForThemeIndex.immune_mucosal: Natural defense peptides, thymus-derived immune modulators, gut barrier repair signals, or anti-inflammatory fragments.
knownForThemeIndex.neuro_mood_sleep: Peptides marketed for thinking, anxiety relief, brain performance, or sleep improvement.
knownForThemeIndex.reproduction_social: Fertility hormones, labor or breastfeeding hormones, or oxytocin-family bonding and social behavior research.
knownForThemeIndex.experimental_weight_adjunct: Non-gut-hormone weight-loss helpers: lipotropic shots, growth-hormone fragment products, research chemicals, or exercise-mimicking compounds sold in metabolic wellness markets.
knownForThemeIndex.aging_bioregulators: Longevity, cell-aging, or ultra-short 'bioregulator' peptides marketed for anti-aging (often with limited Western clinical trial support).

distinctiveQualityLegend.headline_meaning: A plain-language reputation summary: what doctors, researchers, or popular discussion most associate with this type of product. Not a promise that any specific vial delivers on that reputation.
distinctiveQualityLegend.themes_meaning: Themes are intentionally broad groupings (about a dozen) so related products appear together in filters; one product may show up under several themes.
distinctiveQualityLegend.basisNote_meaning: A short note on whether the reputation is backed by major human trials, mixed studies, lab science, marketing, or is simply describing what the product physically is.

reportedBenefitsLegend.prefix_clinical: Based on major human drug-approval trials, large clinical studies, or official prescribing information for a comparable approved drug.
reportedBenefitsLegend.prefix_trial: Based on published human clinical study groups (may be early-stage or small).
reportedBenefitsLegend.prefix_preclinical: Based on animal, cell, or lab-dish experiments; not proof of benefit in people.
reportedBenefitsLegend.prefix_biochemistry: Based on fundamental biology; not a claim about treatment outcomes.
reportedBenefitsLegend.prefix_practice: Based on clinic or wellness practice patterns; controlled outcome studies are often thin.
reportedBenefitsLegend.prefix_vendor: Based on marketing or bundled product framing without a major combined clinical trial.
reportedBenefitsLegend.prefix_human_experience: Based on case reports, surveys, or informal human use stories (weakest evidence tier).
reportedBenefitsLegend.caveat: Each line summarizes a reported benefit for educational purposes, not a promise that a research-use vial will reproduce clinical trial results. Product identity, purity, and medical supervision determine real outcomes.

entries.cyclingNotes: Doses are gradually increased under medical supervision, not cycled on and off. Stopping suddenly in diabetes can cause blood sugar to worsen without a transition plan.

doseGuidelines.minimumEffectiveDoseNotes: Start at a low dose and increase every 4 weeks. Many people stay on 0.5 mg or 1 mg once weekly for blood sugar control. The starting doses are ramp-up steps, not the final effective dose. Check the current prescribing information for exact numbers.
Trials ramped up to 2.4 mg once weekly for maximum weight loss. Lower doses still beat placebo, but 2.4 mg weekly was the target maintenance dose after the ramp-up period.

entries.dosingTimingNotes: Injected under the skin once a week on any chosen day. The oral version has different rules. Research-grade vials sold online are not the same as the pharmacy product.
entries.notes: Exact mapping of the vendor code "1G-SGT" to semaglutide is not confirmed in published scientific research by that string; summary describes semaglutide as the widely used GLP-1 drug class.

potentialApplications.evidenceNote: Strong trial data for pharmacy semaglutide; identity of research vials must match.
potentialApplications.personCenteredBenefit: Help people with type 2 diabetes bring blood sugar down when used as prescribed GLP-1 therapy.

potentialApplications.evidenceNote: Strong controlled trial evidence for semaglutide; not interchangeable with unverified powders.
potentialApplications.personCenteredBenefit: Help some adults lose a meaningful amount of weight combined with diet and movement support.

potentialApplications.evidenceNote: SELECT-style cardiovascular trial context for semaglutide class.
potentialApplications.personCenteredBenefit: May lower risk of major heart events in certain high-risk adults (trial-defined group).

reportedBenefits: Clinical trials: significantly lowers blood sugar (HbA1c) in people with type 2 diabetes.
Clinical trials (STEP program): produces meaningful weight loss when combined with diet and exercise.
Heart health (SELECT trial): reduced major heart events in people with obesity and heart disease.
Also reported: modest drops in blood pressure and cholesterol in trial participants.
How it works: slows stomach emptying and reduces appetite, leading to eating less and better blood sugar control.
entries.researchSummary: A weekly injectable in the same family as Ozempic (semaglutide). It mimics a natural gut hormone called GLP-1 that tells your brain you are full and helps your body manage blood sugar. Large clinical trials showed meaningful weight loss and better blood sugar control. The vendor code "1G-SGT" is shorthand; always confirm the actual contents with a certificate of analysis.

synergisticWith.rationale: Semaglutide is being combined with cagrilintide in a phase 3 drug program called CagriSema, pairing a gut-hormone drug with a fullness-signaling drug for stronger weight loss than either alone.

distinctiveQuality.headline: Best known as a semaglutide-class weight-loss and blood-sugar drug, one of the most widely prescribed injectable peptides in the world.
distinctiveQuality.basisNote: Based on major human trials and approved drug labels; the vendor's code name is not a separate scientific claim.

entries.cyclingNotes: Same as other prescription weight-loss injections: doses are increased slowly to manage nausea. Stopping suddenly can be risky for people with diabetes.

doseGuidelines.indicationOrContext: Type 2 diabetes or weight loss (pharmacy tirzepatide)
doseGuidelines.minimumEffectiveDoseNotes: Start at 2.5 mg once weekly and increase monthly. The first dose is mainly to build tolerance. The effective maintenance doses in trials were in the mid-range of available strengths. Check the current Mounjaro or Zepbound label for the exact schedule.

entries.dosingTimingNotes: Injected under the skin once a week with gradual dose increases. Follow the label for timing around meals.
entries.notes: Vendor "2G-TZ" is widely described in retail copy as a tirzepatide-class dual agonist; confirm identity with the supplier certificate of analysis for your vial.

potentialApplications.evidenceNote: Robust dual GIP/GLP-1 trial data at approved doses.
potentialApplications.personCenteredBenefit: Improve blood sugar control in type 2 diabetes when the molecule is pharmacy tirzepatide used as directed.

potentialApplications.evidenceNote: Trial-supported for tirzepatide where licensed.
potentialApplications.personCenteredBenefit: Support substantial weight loss in obesity when used as prescribed.

reportedBenefits: Clinical trials: lowers blood sugar more effectively than many single-target diabetes drugs.
Clinical trials (SURMOUNT program): produces greater weight loss than older single-pathway drugs, including in head-to-head comparisons.
Also reported: reductions in waist size and improvements in heart-risk blood markers.
How it works: activates two gut-hormone receptors (GIP and GLP-1) for stronger appetite and blood sugar control than either alone.
entries.researchSummary: A weekly injectable related to Mounjaro/Zepbound (tirzepatide). It works on two gut-hormone pathways (GIP and GLP-1) instead of one, which in trials produced even larger drops in weight and blood sugar than older single-pathway drugs. Doses are raised gradually to manage side effects like nausea.

distinctiveQuality.headline: Best known as a dual-action weight-loss and diabetes drug (tirzepatide class) that targets two hormone receptors at once.
distinctiveQuality.basisNote: Based on major human trials for tirzepatide; always confirm that the vial contents match the pharmacy drug.

reportedBenefits: Phase 2 trials: produced some of the largest average weight-loss numbers reported for any drug in this class.
Also reported: improvements in fasting blood sugar and insulin levels at higher doses.
Early data: positive shifts in triglycerides and liver enzyme markers in some participants.

entries.researchSummary: An investigational injectable (retatrutide class) that hits three hormone receptors: GIP, GLP-1, and glucagon. Early trials reported some of the largest average weight reductions seen for any drug in this space. Still in development and not yet approved anywhere.

entries.researchSummary: A small research molecule (not actually a peptide) that targets an enzyme called NNMT, which plays a role in how fat cells handle energy. Showed promising fat-reduction results in mice, but has not been tested in proper human trials. Sold as a research chemical.

doseGuidelines.minimumEffectiveDoseNotes: Doses are protocol-specific, using microgram-per-kilogram infusions in some inflammation trials. There is no consumer dosing guide.

entries.dosingTimingNotes: Given as IV infusions or inhaled in clinical research settings. The route and timing are highly specific to the condition being studied.

potentialApplications.evidenceNote: Investigational; formulation and route decide whether any benefit appears.
potentialApplications.personCenteredBenefit: Explored to cool overactive immunity in gut and lung disease while protecting tissue barriers.

reportedBenefits: Medical relevance: used to understand and treat VIPoma tumors that overproduce this hormone, causing severe diarrhea and electrolyte imbalances.
Lung research: shows anti-inflammatory effects in animal models of lung injury and has been explored for severe asthma.
Gut barrier: lab studies suggest it helps stabilize the protective lining of the intestine.
entries.researchSummary: Vasoactive intestinal peptide is a natural 28-amino-acid hormone involved in gut function, blood vessel dilation, and immune regulation. Clinically relevant in VIPoma tumors that overproduce it. Being researched for lung inflammation and gut barrier protection.

distinctiveQuality.headline: A natural gut-and-immune hormone studied for lung inflammation and intestinal barrier health, known from VIPoma biology.
distinctiveQuality.basisNote: Injecting it into the body is very different from the experimental airway-focused delivery studied in labs.

entries.cyclingNotes: Follow the more conservative schedule of the two components. Note that both BPC-157 and TB-500 are WADA-banned substances.

doseGuidelines.minimumEffectiveDoseNotes: Check the certificate of analysis for the actual milligram split between ingredients, then compare each to its own published research. There is no combined minimum effective dose.

entries.dosingTimingNotes: Combines two short-acting peptides with different timing needs. No unified evidence-based schedule exists for the combination.

potentialApplications.evidenceNote: Mechanism stack only; both parts are banned in many sports and not FDA lifestyle drugs.
potentialApplications.personCenteredBenefit: Popular for athletes wanting layered repair signals after joint, muscle, or connective tissue stress.

reportedBenefits: Combines BPC-157 and TB-500: each has its own animal evidence for tissue repair and healing.
Vendor marketing: positioned as a maximum-recovery product for athletes and injury recovery.
No combined trial data: the side effects and interactions of these two peptides together at high doses have not been formally studied.
entries.researchSummary: A vendor-named product that combines high doses of BPC-157 and TB-500, two popular tissue-repair peptides, in one vial. Named for the pop-culture reference to rapid healing. Each ingredient has animal evidence individually, but the combination has no clinical trial data as a pair.

synergisticWith.rationale: Wolverine products pair BPC-157 with TB-500, combining two different repair mechanisms in one recovery-focused product.

distinctiveQuality.headline: A high-dose healing peptide combo (BPC-157 + TB-500) named after pop culture, with no clinical data for the combined product.
distinctiveQuality.basisNote: Named after a pop-culture healing reference; clinical evidence exists for each ingredient separately, not for the combination.
